correct(theory): the "continuous physics" operator is EXACT INTEGER — the discrete/continuous dichotomy I ran all session is false
Will's correction: I have been sorting operators into "discrete QA" vs "continuous mathematics"
and treating float64 as the real physics. That is wrong. There is no continuous computation --
you cannot perform an infinite calculation; irrational constants are truncated mantissas;
"infinite" functions are never evaluated. The "continuous" time-reversal matched filter that got
AUC 0.97 ran on int16 PCM samples through a 52-bit float truncation over finitely many lags -- a
discretization, just a finer one.

qa_integer_matched_filter.py proves it by construction: the SAME time-reversal operator in PURE
INTEGER arithmetic -- integer PCM samples, integer cross-correlation (sum of products), and the
AUC ranking decided by EXACT integer cross-multiplication (peak_a^2 Ec Ed vs peak_c^2 Ea Eb; no
sqrt, no division, no float) -- gives AUC 0.96, matching the "continuous" 0.97.

So the operator was never continuous. The discrete-QA-vs-continuous-physics framing I ran the
whole session (golden "lives in the continuum", QA "approximates" the continuum, float64 as
"the physics") is FALSE: both sides are finite discrete arithmetic; one just used more of the
signal at finer resolution. The working operator is a discrete integer operator QA can simply
OWN. This is the project's own Wildberger finitism -- exact finite arithmetic is primary, the
continuum is an idealization no computation touches -- which my continuum-privileging contradicted.
That privileging was the fundamental error under the retraction saga and the acoustic framing,
not a detail.

Honest through-line (consistent with the whole session): the part that works is the standard

author: Player3
content:
feat(experiment): scored exact-vs-float on a real reversible scramble — exact wins past N=40, ties below
A real scored task where exact-integer QA can WIN or LOSE against float64: reversible data
scrambling with the Arnold cat map (a genuine image-encryption scheme). Round-trip a real byte
block (GEMINI.md, 100x100) forward-N + inverse-N and score byte-exact recovery.

RESULT (fair fight, both sides shown):
  N <= 30 : float64 recovers 100% (TIES exact) -> exactness UNNECESSARY, it does not win there.
  N  = 40 : float64 byte recovery collapses 100% -> 2.4% while exact stays 100%.
  N >= 60 : float64 ~2% (data destroyed); exact byte-perfect at every N.

Mechanism: the cat map is a PERMUTATION on the integer grid, so exact forward-then-inverse is
the identity (100% by construction). In float64 the continuous positions drift at the Lyapunov
rate; past ~N=40 the drift exceeds one cell (predicted ~33) and rounds to the wrong pixel. The
byte-recovery cliff (100%->2.4%) is far sharper than the position drift (100%->98%) because
float collisions BREAK the bijection -- two pixels round to one cell, a byte is silently lost --
so a 2% position error destroys 98% of the data.

So QA's exact-integer rule is neither a purist tic nor always needed: it ties float for short
computations (where it can lose) and genuinely WINS a scored task (lossless recovery) for
reversible/long/chaotic ones, with the horizon shortening as the grid gets finer. Real signal
processing agrees -- lossless codecs (JPEG2000 integer lifting) use exact integer arithmetic
for exactly this reason. Committed prediction (float fails ~N=33) confirmed (cliff at 40).

feat(experiment): non-unit QA on real ERA5 climate — real data is non-unit (dissipative), golden unit is the special case
The first FALSIFIABLE test of the arc against real data (not a theorem check). Defines
"non-unit QA" operationally: a QA whose orbit generator (companion of its 2nd-order
recurrence x_n=a*x_{n-1}+b*x_{n-2}) has |det|=|b|!=1 -- dissipative, order at the p-adic
(discrete) place (Phase P/Q) -- vs the golden QA whose generator is a UNIT (|b|=1,
conservative, order at the archimedean/observer place, Fibonacci a=b=1). The unit/non-unit
dichotomy thus maps to a MEASURABLE property: conservative vs dissipative dynamics.

COMMITTED PREDICTION (no hedge): real climate signals are dissipative -> NON-unit (|b|<1),
not the golden unit. Tested on ERA5 (3297 daily records, 15 geopotential/temp/wind series).

RESULT -- prediction CONFIRMED: all 15 series are NON-unit (median |det|=0.13, roots deep
inside the unit circle, strongly dissipative), AR(2) fits are real (median R^2=0.61 vs
shuffle-null R^2=0.00), and forcing the best UNIT generator |b|=1 (either sign) costs 0.28
R^2 -> the data rejects the whole unit class. So the golden/unit QA is the special
CONSERVATIVE (measure-zero, on-the-unit-circle) case; the generic real signal is the
non-unit archetype -- "non-unit QA" is the empirically relevant one.

HONEST CAVEATS (in the artifact): (1) stationarity alone forces |det|<1, so the non-unit
DIRECTION was a partly-safe bet -- the real content is the LARGE distance from unit and the
0.28 R^2 rejection, not the sign. (2) This shows real data is non-conservative; it does NOT
show any golden-SPECIFIC (phi) signature -- that remains open and untested.

Codex caught three real issues, all fixed: |b|>1 is expansive not dissipative (label split);
the companion determinant is -b not b (header); the unit CLASS is |b|=1 either sign, so the
constrained comparison now tests b=+1 AND b=-1 and takes the best (gap dropped 0.52->0.28,
the fair number). Also acknowledges my earlier error (conflating Pisano period 24 of mod-9
with a modulus 24) that this experiment supersedes.
